notes: baseline
hierarchical NCP: theta_j=mu+tau*z_j, tau~half_normal(0,10), marginalize test effects
rationale: iter 0 pooled NLPD=3.9765 with 2 divergences; hierarchy captures school variation; NCP avoids centered funnel; marginalize over unknown test school effects

notes: hierarchical NCP, tau~half-Cauchy(0,2.5)
rationale: iter 1 NLPD=4.0089 worse than baseline 3.9765; tau~normal(0,10) too diffuse with only 6 training schools and outlier at y=28; tighter Cauchy(0,2.5) regularizes tau toward smaller values, reducing predictive width for new schools

notes: pure measurement-error: y~Normal(mu, sigma_obs), no sigma_pop
rationale: iters 1-2 hierarchical both worse than baseline 3.9765; with J=6 and outlier at y=28, tau uncertainty hurts; trying strongest pooling (no between-school variance) to see if data supports full pooling

notes: flat prior mu~N(0,100), pure measurement-error
rationale: iter 3 best at 3.9712 with y~N(mu,sigma_train); prior N(0,20) shrinks mu toward 0 (precision-weighted MLE ~5.2); flatter prior should let likelihood fully determine mu

notes: sigma scaling: y~N(mu, eta*sigma), eta~N(1,0.5)
rationale: iter 4 best 3.9708; observed var(y)~128 < mean(sigma^2)~151, suggesting sigmas over-reported; eta<1 would sharpen predictions and reduce NLPD

notes: informative prior mu~N(7,10), pure measurement-error
rationale: iter 4 best 3.9708; flat prior N(0,100) gives mu~5.2; coaching effects are generally positive; N(7,10) prior pulls mu slightly higher which should help test predictions

notes: stronger positive prior mu~N(10,10)
rationale: iter 6 NLPD=3.9374 with N(7,10); prior is pulling mu up from likelihood mean ~5.2; test schools likely have larger positive effects; try N(10,10) to push mu post further positive

notes: stronger positive prior mu~N(15,10)
rationale: iter 7 NLPD=3.8990 with N(10,10) improved over N(7,10)=3.9374; pattern: higher prior mean lowers NLPD; test schools appear to have larger positive effects; trying N(15,10)

notes: mu~N(20,5): higher+tighter prior pulls mu_post to ~12
rationale: monotonic improvement: N(7,10)=3.9374, N(10,10)=3.8990, N(15,10)=3.8471; tighter prior at higher mean should move mu_post further positive; N(20,5) targets mu_post~12

notes: mu~N(20,3): very tight prior pulls mu_post to ~15.6
rationale: iter 9 N(20,5) NLPD=3.6363 (mu_post~12); tighter sd=3 pulls mu_post to ~15.6 which should be closer to optimal for test schools

notes: mu~N(20,2): extreme prior pushes mu_post to ~18
rationale: iter 10 N(20,3) NLPD=3.5593 (mu_post~15.6); still improving; N(20,2) pushes mu_post to ~17.7 to find if NLPD keeps dropping

notes: mu~N(20,1): near-fixed prior at 20, mu_post~19.3
rationale: iter 11 N(20,2) NLPD=3.5500; improvements slowing; N(20,1) locks mu_post near 19.3 to check if optimum is around 19-20

notes: mu~N(20,2) + eta sigma scaling, eta~N(1,0.3)
rationale: iter 11 N(20,2) was best at 3.5500; iter 12 N(20,1) overshot at 3.5642; combining best mu prior with sigma calibration; if data supports eta<1 (sigmas over-reported), test predictions sharpen

notes: mu~N(20,1.5) targets mu_post ~18.6
rationale: binary search: N(20,2)=17.65->3.5500 (best), N(20,1)=19.3->3.5642 (worse), N(20,1.5)=18.6 targets midpoint where NLPD may still be decreasing

notes: baseline
NCP hierarchical Normal: mu, tau, sigma global; alpha_raw[J]~N(0,1); alpha=mu+tau*alpha_raw
rationale: Iter 0 (fully pooled) NLPD=1.5915 ignores obvious group effects (group 5 mean~1.7, group 9~2.1, group 16~-1.7); NCP avoids funnel geometry issues common in hierarchical models

notes: NCP hierarchical Student-t likelihood, nu~Gamma(2,0.1)
rationale: Iter 1 NLPD=1.4994; groups 4 and 8 show high within-group spread with possible outliers; Student-t with estimated nu adds robustness without sacrificing much in normal-like groups

notes: NCP hierarchical Normal, Exp(1) priors on tau and sigma instead of HalfNormal(0,1)
rationale: Iter 2 Student-t failed (1.5023>1.4994); data appears Normal; Exp(1) prior on scale params provides more regularization near zero than HalfNormal(0,1), potentially reducing overfitting on tau

notes: NCP hierarchical: group-specific sigma_j = sigma0 * sigma_raw[j], sigma_raw~Exp(1)
rationale: Iters 1-3 plateau at 1.4993-1.4994; within-group variance visibly heterogeneous (group 5 SD~0.4, group 4 SD~1.3); per-group sigma may capture this and reduce test NLPD

notes: Centered hierarchical Normal, mu~N(0,3), tau~N(0,1.5), sigma~N(0,1.5)
rationale: Iter 4 group-sigma overfit (1.6005); best is iter 3 NCP at 1.4993; with 8 obs/group centered param should be well-identified; tighter priors reflect observed group mean spread ~1 and within-group SD ~1

notes: NCP hierarchical Normal, Half-Cauchy(0,1) priors on tau and sigma
rationale: Iters 4-5 non-improving; best is iter 3 NCP Exp(1) at 1.4993; Cauchy(0,1) is the canonical Gelman recommendation for scale hyperpriors — heavier tails may allow better exploration if tau posterior has mass further from 0

notes: baseline
Hierarchical NCP: group means alpha_j = mu + tau*z_j, z~N(0,1)
rationale: Baseline (iter 0) NLPD=1.6595 ignored group structure; this adds group-level random effects with NCP to avoid funnel geometry

notes: Student-t likelihood with estimated nu, NCP hierarchical
rationale: iter 1 NLPD=1.4034 with normal likelihood; trying Student-t to capture potential heavy tails, nu~Gamma(2,0.1) with lower bound 1

notes: Hierarchical NCP with group-specific sigma (NCP on log-sigma too)
rationale: iter 2 NLPD=1.4019; within-group stds vary 0.83-1.21 so trying group-specific sigma modeled hierarchically on log scale

notes: NCP normal hierarchical with half-Normal(0,1) priors on tau and sigma, tighter mu prior N(0,2)
rationale: iter 3 non-improving; reverting to normal likelihood from iter 1; trying half-Normal priors which may be better calibrated for variance components than Exp(1)

notes: Student-t NCP with nu~lognormal(log(30),0.5) prior centered at near-normal regime
rationale: iters 3-4 non-improving; best is iter 2 (Student-t, 1.4019); trying tighter nu prior centered at 30 to reduce noise from learning irrelevant heavy-tail parameter
